name: Dialog
description: A dialog is a modal window for a focused task or decision that interrupts the operator on purpose — confirming a deletion, capturing a few fields, or surfacing something that needs a choice before continuing.
keyRule: Use a dialog only when the task genuinely needs to interrupt. For routine detail, prefer a side sheet or a detail page. For transient confirmation, prefer a snackbar.

variants.name: Form dialog
variants.whenToUse: Capture a few fields without leaving the page.

variants.name: Full-screen (rare on desktop)
variants.whenToUse: A larger task that benefits from focus; usually a page is better.

anatomy.name: Scrim
anatomy.description: dimmed overlay that blocks the page behind.

anatomy.name: Container
anatomy.description: the dialog surface (4px radius, high elevation).

anatomy.name: Title
anatomy.description: a short, specific question or task name.

behaviors.name: Modality
behaviors.description: The dialog traps focus and blocks the page; Escape and the scrim dismiss non-destructive dialogs.

behaviors.name: Action order
behaviors.description: Dismiss on the left, confirm on the right; destructive confirms use the error colour and clear labels ("Remove", not "OK").

behaviors.name: Keep it short
behaviors.description: One decision or a handful of fields; longer tasks belong on a page.

states.treatment: Surface over scrim; focus trapped inside.

props.name: open
props.description: Controls whether the dialog is rendered/open.

props.name: title
props.description: Short, specific question or task name (e.g. "Remove vehicle?"), not a generic title.

props.name: confirmLabel
props.description: Right-aligned, rendered last. Verb-first label (e.g. "Remove"), not "OK".

props.name: destructive
props.description: Destructive confirms use the error colour and disable scrim/Escape dismissal so an unsaved destructive action isn't lost silently.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called on Escape / scrim click for non-destructive dialogs. Defaults to onCancel.

doThis: Use a specific title that states the decision.
Label actions with verbs (Remove/Save), not OK.
Trap focus and return it on close.
dontDoThis: Don't use a dialog for routine detail — use a side sheet/page.
Don't let the scrim dismiss a destructive, unsaved action silently.
accessibility: Use role=dialog with aria-modal and a labelled title.
Trap focus while open and restore it to the trigger on close.
Action buttons have clear, discernible names.

related.name: SideSheet
related.why: Complex forms that need page context stay in a sheet
